summary: First testing run — comprehensive backend (44 pytest cases) and frontend (auth + dashboards + catalog) testing of LMS. Backend 42/44 passed (95%); frontend smoke tests passed for all 3 role dashboards, login/logout, wrong-creds (no React crash), catalog filters render. One real backend bug: brute-force lockout doesn't trigger behind proxy.

critical.issue: Brute-force lockout NEVER triggers behind ingress proxy. `request.client.host` returns the upstream pod IP which varies per request (observed 10.208.130.77, 10.208.130.78 in db.login_attempts). Since identifier = `{ip}:{email}`, each request creates/updates a different identifier and count never reaches MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS=5. Secondary issue: MongoDB returns timezone-naive datetimes; comparing `locked_until > datetime.now(timezone.utc)` will TypeError once count finally reaches 5 on a stable IP. Fix: use email-only identifier (or X-Forwarded-For) and `locked_until.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)` before compare.

minor.issue: Returns 404 (route `@api.get('/')` becomes `/api/` but trailing-slash redirect not configured). Harmless.
Confusing one-liner on line 253 of server.py uses ternary precedence that is hard to read; works but should be refactored for clarity.

issues: Console warning: 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0'. ResponsiveContainer parent likely has no explicit height in some mount cycle. Charts still render after rehydration but the warning indicates flicker on first paint.
Showed 'Loading...' for >1s after navigation; consider adding skeleton cards instead of bare text.

action_items: Fix brute-force lockout identifier (use email-only or X-Forwarded-For) and make locked_until comparison tz-aware in /app/backend/server.py login() function.
Optional: refactor confusing category lookup ternary in list_courses (server.py:253).
Optional: add `minHeight` to chart ResponsiveContainer parents in EducatorDashboard.jsx & AdminDashboard.jsx to suppress recharts width=-1 warning.
Optional: replace 'Loading...' text on admin dashboard with skeleton component.
critical_code_review_comments: Brute-force lockout is effectively disabled in production due to ephemeral pod IPs — security regression.

MongoDB datetime fields are stored timezone-naive (motor strips tz). Multiple places in server.py compare naive vs aware datetimes (e.g., login lockout, forgot-password token expiry). password_reset has same pattern: `record.get('expires_at') < datetime.now(timezone.utc)` — will TypeError if a valid token is checked. Recommend a helper `as_utc(dt)` used everywhere.
/api/courses route line 253 has a misleading one-liner ternary; ObjectId.is_valid(category) is evaluated AFTER an `or` short-circuit — works by luck for slug-shaped strings.
auth.py: `secure=True, samesite='none'` on cookies — correct for prod cross-site, but means cookies won't be set on plain http://localhost:3000 dev. Documented behavior.
Frontend uses Bearer fallback in localStorage so auth still works even when cookies blocked — good defensive design.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 TEST_ course (deleted at end), 1 TEST_ lockout user, plus a few TEST_ registrations as part of test flow. No permanent pollution.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (pytest, 44 cases, JUnit xml output). Two failures: (1) test_register_new_student asserts uppercase email — backend correctly lowercases, expected; just update assertion. (2) test_brute_force_lockout — REAL BUG, do not just relax the test; fix the login() identifier strategy first. Frontend tested via browser_automation — admin/educator/student dashboards all reachable; recharts warning about width=-1 is cosmetic. Course-builder window.prompt flow and admin Users/Categories CRUD UI were not exercised end-to-end — next agent should cover those.
rca of the issue: Brute-force lockout bug RCA: `request.client.host` inside a Kubernetes pod behind ingress returns the calling pod/node IP, not the originating client IP. Each external request can be load-balanced through a different ingress instance, producing different `request.client.host` values. The identifier `{ip}:{email}` therefore differs across attempts and the per-identifier counter never accumulates. Reproduction: hit /api/auth/login 10× with wrong password, observe `db.login_attempts` has multiple rows each with count=1 or 2. Mitigation: parse `request.headers.get('X-Forwarded-For')` first (take leftmost), fall back to client.host; OR drop IP from identifier entirely (email-only) for MVP.
